frameworks.name: prestimulus brain-state / spontaneous baseline fluctuations
frameworks.one_line: Ongoing pre-stimulus activity biases how an ambiguous stimulus is perceived.

frameworks.name: perceptual bistability / multistable perception
frameworks.one_line: Necker cube reversals used as a probe of subjective perceptual state.

frameworks.name: EEG microstate / scalp-map topography
frameworks.one_line: Spatial configuration of scalp potentials indexes the momentary brain state.

core_question: Does the brain state in the 50 ms before an ambiguous stimulus predict whether perception will reverse?
key_assumptions: Scalp-map topography over a 50 ms window captures the sum of ongoing brain processes
Necker-cube percepts are an adequate readout of perceptual state
Distributed inverse solutions can localize prestimulus generators
manipulations: Intermittent presentation of an ambiguous Necker cube
Trial-by-trial sorting by reported reversal vs. stability
Classification of trials by which of two prestimulus scalp maps was present
key_findings: Two prestimulus scalp maps doubly dissociated reversal trials from stable trials
Inverse-solution contrast localized prereversal activity to right inferior parietal cortex
Prestimulus state predicted subsequent perceptual outcome
interpretations: Perceptual awareness is co-determined by ongoing brain state, not stimulus alone
Right inferior parietal cortex carries a prestimulus signature that gates perceptual reversal
